data.title: Shuanghua Holdings Swings to Gross Loss as Revenue Falls and Net Loss Widens in 2025
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Shuanghua Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1241) ).

Shuanghua Holdings Ltd. reported a sharp decline in revenue to RMB45.5 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, down from RMB82.8 million a year earlier, as higher cost of sales pushed the group into a gross loss of RMB0.6 million versus a prior gross profit. The company’s net loss attributable to shareholders widened to RMB18.8 million from RMB16.9 million, with basic and diluted losses per share increasing to RMB2.9 cents, underscoring sustained operational pressures despite some impairment reversals on receivables and a modest income tax credit.

Other income, gains and losses swung from a positive RMB6.7 million in 2024 to a loss of RMB3.7 million in 2025, compounding weaker top-line performance and offsetting reductions in administrative and selling expenses. The results signal continuing profitability challenges for Shuanghua Holdings, which may weigh on investor sentiment and highlight the need for further cost controls or strategic adjustments to stabilize earnings and protect shareholder value.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1241) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.18 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shuanghua Holdings Ltd. stock, see the HK:1241 Stock Forecast page.

More about Shuanghua Holdings Ltd.

Shuanghua Holdings Ltd., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1241, operates through a group structure that generates revenue from its consolidated subsidiaries. The company reports its financials in Renminbi and appears focused on manufacturing or industrial operations, as indicated by its cost of sales profile and distribution expenses.

Average Trading Volume: 191,500

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Symphony Holdings Limited ( (HK:1223) ) is now available.

Symphony Holdings Limited reported largely flat group revenue of HKD308.8 million for 2025, with its retailing segment edging up to HKD241.5 million, while gross profit and other income provided support to overall performance. The company’s EBITDA more than doubled to HKD120.3 million and finance costs fell significantly, helping narrow the net loss attributable to shareholders by about 77% to HKD32.9 million.

Despite remaining in the red, the marked reduction in losses and improved operating metrics signal a stabilising turnaround, aided by lower impairment charges and a swing to fair value gains on financial assets. The board proposed maintaining a final dividend of HKD0.005 per share, indicating confidence in cash flow and offering some reassurance to shareholders as the group works to further strengthen its financial position.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1223) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Symphony Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:1223 Stock Forecast page.

Great China Holdings (Hong Kong) Limited ( (HK:0021) ) has provided an update.

Great China Holdings (Hong Kong) Limited reported a sharp deterioration in its 2025 results, as revenue fell to HK$60.6 million from HK$132.6 million a year earlier and gross profit dropped accordingly. The company swung to a significantly wider net loss of HK$148.4 million, driven by fair value losses on investment properties, goodwill and property impairments, and a large foreign exchange loss, pushing basic loss per share to HK3.73 cents despite a tax credit and positive exchange translation in other comprehensive income.

The full-year comprehensive loss narrowed to HK$73.2 million from HK$164.0 million due to favourable currency translation, but core operating and valuation pressures intensified, underlining the group’s vulnerability to property market revaluations and currency movements. The results signal continued financial strain that may weigh on shareholder returns and highlight execution and market risks around the company’s asset-heavy portfolio and earnings volatility.

China Parenting Network Holdings Limited ( (HK:1736) ) just unveiled an update.

China Parenting Network Holdings Limited reported revenue of approximately RMB57.5 million for 2025, a modest 1.9% increase from a year earlier, while its net loss widened sharply to about RMB20.2 million, up 282.7% from 2024. The results highlight mounting profitability pressures even as the company maintains top-line growth in a competitive and structurally shifting maternal and child market.

Management described 2025 as a pivotal year for China’s maternal and child industry, which is moving from scale-driven expansion to value-oriented development under demographic headwinds and evolving consumer behavior. Policy support, including new childcare subsidies and expansion of affordable services, is bolstering demand, while trends such as precision nutrition, AI-enabled smart parenting, green consumption and omni-channel integration are reshaping competition and creating both opportunities and execution risks for players like CI Web.

Diwang Industrial Holdings Limited ( (HK:1950) ) has issued an announcement.

Diwang Industrial Holdings Limited reported revenue of approximately RMB599.3 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, a 5.8% rise from 2024, with gross profit up 10.8% to about RMB285.2 million, reflecting improved margins despite higher costs. However, the group swung to a net loss of around RMB1.8 million from a prior-year profit of RMB30.0 million, as sharply higher impairment losses and increased selling and distribution expenses eroded earnings, prompting the board to withhold a final dividend and signaling pressure on profitability despite top-line growth.

Profit attributable to owners of the company dropped to roughly RMB2.5 million from RMB30.0 million a year earlier, and basic and diluted earnings per share fell to RMB0.35 cents from RMB4.17 cents, underscoring a significant deterioration in shareholder returns. The results highlight mounting credit risk costs and operating expense burdens that may weigh on Diwang Industrial’s financial flexibility and could concern investors looking for stable dividends and consistent earnings in a challenging industrial market environment.

data.title: Natural Food International Corrects Rounding Error in 2025 Final Dividend Figure
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Natural Food International Holding Ltd. ( (HK:1837) ).

Natural Food International Holding Ltd. has clarified a rounding discrepancy in its recently published 2025 annual results regarding the proposed final dividend. The board had recommended a final dividend of HK$0.069 per ordinary share for the year ended 31 December 2025, subject to shareholder approval at the upcoming annual general meeting.

In the dividend note to the financial statements, the payout was mistakenly described as HK$7 cents per share due to rounding, which conflicted with the more precise HK$0.069 figure. The company confirmed that the correct proposed dividend is HK$6.9 cents per share, and stressed that, aside from this correction, all other information in the 2025 results announcement remains unchanged, aiming to prevent any confusion for investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1837) stock is a Buy with a HK$1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Natural Food International Holding Ltd. stock, see the HK:1837 Stock Forecast page.

More about Natural Food International Holding Ltd.

The group focuses on branded natural food products, positioning itself in the consumer market for health‑oriented and grain‑based foods in Greater China and beyond.

data.title: Wenye Group Seeks Shareholder Approval After Rule Breach on US$20.25 Million Saipan Deal
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Wenye Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:1802) ).

Wenye Group Holdings disclosed that its subsidiary Longxin signed a US$20.25 million Saipan project contract with The Palette Inc., a company majority-controlled by former non-executive director Chen Li and his son and current executive director Chen Zhouyu, making it a connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules. The company admitted it failed to meet announcement, circular, independent shareholders’ approval and reporting requirements due to a misinterpretation of the rules, and will now convene an extraordinary general meeting, form an independent board committee, appoint an independent financial adviser, and issue a circular by 30 April 2026 so independent shareholders can review and vote on ratifying the deal.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1802) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.04 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Wenye Group Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:1802 Stock Forecast page.

More about Wenye Group Holdings Limited

Wenye Group Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the construction and engineering sector, undertaking project contracts through its subsidiaries. The group focuses on building and infrastructure works and related services, serving both domestic and overseas markets such as Saipan through project-based arrangements.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Yunnan Jinxun Resources Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3636) ) has issued an announcement.

Yunnan Jinxun Resources Co., Ltd. has declared a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.33 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, equivalent to HKD 0.373371 per share based on an exchange rate of RMB 1 to HKD 1.131427. The dividend is subject to shareholder approval on 18 May 2026, with the shares trading ex-dividend from 17 June, a record date of 24 June, and payment scheduled for 10 July 2026.

The company detailed the withholding tax framework for H-share investors with registered addresses outside the PRC, applying a 10% enterprise income tax for non-resident enterprises and generally a 10% individual income tax, rising to 20% for residents of jurisdictions without favorable tax treaties. These arrangements clarify post-tax returns for different categories of overseas shareholders and may influence after-tax yield considerations for international investors in the stock.

An update from CHK Oil ( (HK:0632) ) is now available.

CHK Oil Limited plans to amend its existing bye-laws to align them with recent changes to Hong Kong Listing Rules on electronic corporate communications, hybrid meetings, electronic voting and proxy addresses, and to bring them into line with Bermuda law. The amendments will also enable the company to hold treasury shares, accommodate the uncertificated securities market regime, and introduce various consequential and housekeeping changes, all of which are subject to shareholder approval by special resolution at the forthcoming annual general meeting.

A circular detailing the proposed changes to the bye-laws and the adoption of a new set of bye-laws, together with the notice of the annual general meeting, will be sent to shareholders in due course. If approved, the revisions are expected to modernise CHK Oil’s corporate governance framework and offer greater operational flexibility in how it manages shareholder communication, meeting formats and share capital structure.

Minieye Technology reported 2025 revenue of RMB759.0 million, a 16.0% year-on-year increase, with gross profit rising to RMB141.4 million and margin improving from 16.0% to 18.6% after cost-control and efficiency measures. Despite this, the company remained loss-making at the operating and net levels, underscoring the heavy investment phase of its autonomous driving and intelligent vehicle technologies.

A key milestone was the first full year of commercialization for its driverless vehicles and operational services, which generated about RMB65.0 million and helped establish a dual-pillar model of L2+ mass-production for OEMs and L4 large-scale driverless operations. The group reported rapid progress in driverless logistics and autonomous minibuses, securing cooperation involving over 6,000 logistics vehicles and more than ten autonomous minibus projects, including deployment at Hubei Ezhou Airport and as an official vehicle partner for the 2025 World Internet Conference, positioning it as an early mover in scalable autonomous mobility solutions.

CHK Oil ( (HK:0632) ) just unveiled an announcement.

CHK Oil Limited reported a sharp decline in revenue to HK$58.8 million for 2025 from HK$153.1 million a year earlier, but swung from a loss to a profit as it booked substantial reversals of previous impairments on property, plant, equipment and intangible assets. The group posted a net profit of HK$16.2 million versus a HK$21.5 million loss in 2024, with improved other income and lower administrative expenses helping offset weaker sales, resulting in positive earnings per share and signalling a partial recovery in its balance sheet and financial position despite ongoing top-line pressure.

Yunnan Jinxun Resources Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3636) ) just unveiled an update.

Yunnan Jinxun Resources Co., Ltd. reported strong growth for the year ended 31 December 2025, with revenue rising 28.3% to RMB2.27 billion and gross profit climbing 41.1% to RMB518.9 million. The company’s gross profit margin improved to 22.8%, and basic earnings per share increased to RMB2.98, underscoring improved operational efficiency and profitability.

Net profit surged 62.4% year on year to RMB328.8 million, lifting the net margin to 14.5% and highlighting the company’s strengthened earnings profile. data.title: Chiho Environmental Reports Stable Operations as It Advances Toward Trading Resumption
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Chiho Environmental Group Limited ( (HK:0976) ).

Chiho Environmental Group Limited, a major metal recycling company with significant operations across Asia and Europe, says its core recycling and related businesses remain broadly stable and are operating as usual in all material respects. The group, which plays a notable role in the international scrap metal and resource recovery market, notes that neither the identified irregularities at certain subsidiaries nor the continued suspension of trading have so far caused adverse operational disruption, based on the board’s preliminary assessment.

The company provided a quarterly update on its efforts to meet resumption requirements set by the Hong Kong Stock Exchange following the trading halt linked to those irregularities. It has submitted draft reports on an independent forensic investigation and an internal control review to the exchange, a key step toward satisfying resumption guidance and demonstrating improved governance, with further disclosures on findings and remedial actions to follow.

More about Chiho Environmental Group Limited

Chiho Environmental Group Limited is one of the world’s largest publicly listed metal recycling corporations, with operations spanning Asia and Europe. The group focuses on recycling and processing scrap metals, positioning itself as a key player in the circular economy and resource recovery value chain, and reports that its core businesses continue to run normally despite recent governance issues.

The company’s operational footprint and metal recycling activities have, according to its directors, not been materially affected by previously disclosed irregularities at certain subsidiaries or by the prolonged suspension of trading in its shares. Management continues to monitor any potential impact from these issues and has committed to updating investors as necessary.
